metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_155
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This publication assigns Collybistin immunoreactivity clearly to a subset of inhibitory synapses throughout the brain. Collybistin is an established interactor of Gephyrin, which was localized to the inhibitory postsynapse through immunogold as reviewed in 24552784.

10/2/2017 Pim
Note: clusters of gephyrin, a postsynaptic protein, are used in this publication as a proxy for inh. synapses (see PMID:24552784).
Fig.2: inh. (GABA) synapses in cerebellar ctx [rat]
Fig.3: inh. (GABA) synapses in purkinje cells [mouse]
Fig.4: various brain area's in brain [rat]
Fig.5: colocalization with different GABA-A-R subunits in various brain area's, and with GlycRs in brainstem [rat]
My final interpretation is that CB3 is present in the postsynaptic specialization (using gephyrin as a proxy) of GABA-ergic and Glycin-ergic synapses in the rodent brain (as also indicated by the Gundelfinger group).. Experimental description: This study was performed on adult (4-8 weeks old) Wistar rats (n=14) and C57BL/6J mice (n=5) of both sexes.

10/2/2017 Pim: anntotation against Rat approved.
